summary: Assessed DinoBrain as a potential compounding knowledge OS. Conclusion: the architecture has the right foundation for compounding user-specific knowledge, but sustained use alone does not guarantee compounding. It needs enforced live pre-response hooks, reliable session distillation, reviewed promotion into accepted behavior/wiki/source records, retrieval quality, behavior evaluation, lifecycle pruning, and gated sync/restore to become a true compounding OS rather than a log accumulator.
decisions: Classify current DinoBrain as a compounding-capable foundation, not a fully autonomous compounding OS yet.
Define true compounding as repeated conversion of conversations into reviewed preferences, decisions, claims, corrections, and behavior rules that measurably improve future sessions.
next_steps: Fix live version drift so the running MCP reports v2.0.2 after reload/restart.
Implement or enforce a session distillation and promotion loop that turns every completed task into candidate memories, then reviewed accepted records.
Add behavior eval to prove memory-on sessions outperform memory-off sessions over time.
